problem.title: Intersection of Two Arrays II
problem.description: Given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, return an array of their intersection. Each element in the result must appear as many times as it shows in both arrays (not just once). The result can be in any order.
assumptions: Each element in the result appears min(count in nums1, count in nums2) times.
Follow-up: what if arrays are sorted? what if one array is much larger?

example.explanation: 2 appears twice in both arrays so the intersection contains [2,2]. Use a frequency map: count each element in nums1, then for each element in nums2 decrement the count and add to result if count > 0.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – HashMap Frequency Count
solutions.description: Count the frequency of each element in nums1 using a HashMap. Then iterate nums2: whenever an element has a remaining count > 0 in the map, add it to the result and decrement the count. This handles duplicates correctly because we consume one occurrence per match.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Two Pointers (Sorted)
solutions.description: If both arrays are sorted, use two pointers. Advance the pointer with the smaller value; when they match, add to result and advance both. This is optimal when arrays are already sorted and avoids extra space.

complexity.time: O(m log m + n log n)
